2606.30265.title: When Is a Draft Accepted? A Theory of Acceptance in Speculative Decoding
2606.30265.paper_summary: Speculative decoding accelerates language model inference by using a fast drafter to propose candidate tokens that are then verified by a larger target model. Existing theory largely studies the stochastic, distribution-preserving setting, where the goal is to exactly sample from the target distribution. In contrast, many practical systems use greedy decoding, relaxed acceptance rules, or tree-based candidate sets, where success is governed by local ranking and threshold events rather than exact distributional equality. We develop a theory for these regimes. We identify that many common acceptance criteria have rejection regions that can be characterized as lower level sets of the target distribution. For these, we characterize the exact KL divergence required for rejection yielding exact certificates and sharp margin-based bounds for strict greedy decoding, additive and multiplicative relaxed acceptance, top-(m) relaxed criteria, and entropy-thresholded acceptance. We then extend the framework to greedy tree decoding, deriving exact and margin-only certificates for when the target greedy token remains covered by the drafter's top-(m) candidates. Finally, we evaluate the resulting certificates on Qwen3 models, showing that relaxed and tree-based criteria substantially enlarge the region of certified acceptance, especially on decoding steps with low target model distribution margin. These results complement existing distribution-preserving analyses of speculative decoding by characterizing the deterministic local acceptance events common in practical inference systems.
2606.30265.paper_summary_zh: 推測解碼透過使用快速草擬者提出候選標記，然後由較大的目標模型進行驗證，來加速語言模型推理。現有理論主要研究隨機分佈保持設定，目標是從目標分佈中精確取樣。相較之下，許多實際系統使用貪婪解碼、放鬆的接受規則或基於樹的候選集，成功是由局部排名和閾值事件所主導，而非精確的分佈平等。我們為這些情況發展了一套理論。我們識別出許多常見的接受標準具有可以被表徵為目標分佈的下層集合的拒絕區域。對於這些，我們表徵了拒絕所需的精確KL散度，從而產生精確的證明和基於邊界的嚴格貪婪解碼、加法和乘法放鬆接受、前-(m)放鬆標準以及熵閾接受的尖銳邊界。我們接著將該框架擴展至貪婪樹解碼，推導出當目標貪婪標記仍然被草擬者的前-(m)候選所覆蓋時的精確和僅基於邊界的證明。最後，我們在Qwen3模型上評估所產生的證明，顯示放鬆和基於樹的標準大幅擴大了認證接受的區域，特別是在目標模型分佈邊界較低的解碼步驟中。這些結果補充了現有的推測解碼分佈保持分析，通過表徵實際推理系統中常見的確定性局部接受事件。
2606.30265.author: Aaryam Sharma et.al.
